title: thapsus × thapsoides
intro: Thapsus × thapsoides, also known as Thapsia or Thapsia garganica, is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence. Thapsus × thapsoides contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
